channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Roosevelt Institute Graduate Humanities Internship 2026–2027: Paid Remote Opportunity for Policy, Research and Public Service Careers
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The Roosevelt Institute is proud to announce the Graduate Humanities Internship, a unique paid, remote opportunity designed to bridge the gap between humanities scholarship and public service careers. Supported by the Mellon Foundation, this program provides graduate students with practical experience in policy research, grant writing, communications, and other professional skills to help humanities scholars […]

The post Roosevelt Institute Graduate Humanities Internship 2026–2027: Paid Remote Opportunity for Policy, Research and Public Service Careers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Roosevelt Institute is proud to announce the Graduate Humanities Internship, a unique paid, remote opportunity designed to bridge the gap between humanities scholarship and public service careers. Supported by the Mellon Foundation, this program provides graduate students with practical experience in policy research, grant writing, communications, and other professional skills to help humanities scholars translate their expertise into meaningful public service work.

Why This Internship Matters

The Roosevelt Institute Graduate Humanities Internship is ideal for students from diverse humanities backgrounds—whether your focus is medieval art history, modern ethical theory, literary studies, or other fields. This internship equips you to connect your research skills to real-world policy challenges, giving you hands-on experience in sectors where analytical thinking, writing, and research are highly valued.

Internship Structure

  • Duration: Two semesters—Fall 2026 and Spring 2027, each 12 weeks long.
  • Time Commitment: 10–16 hours/week, with flexibility to align with your academic schedule.
  • Stipend: $25/hour.
  • Format: Primarily remote; hybrid options available for students in the DC or NYC metro areas.

Semester 1: Foundations and Exposure

Interns will rotate through Roosevelt Institute departments, gaining experience with:

  • Development
  • Communications
  • Government Relations
  • Research

This semester introduces students to organizational operations and provides opportunities to build foundational skills in policy-related work.

Semester 2: Research and Policy Application

Interns will collaborate with Roosevelt staff to apply their humanities expertise to current policy questions. With guidance from project mentors, students will develop a capstone project, which may include:

  • Policy briefs
  • Grant proposals
  • Policy strategy documents
  • Press engagement plans

These projects will directly contribute to ongoing initiatives at the Roosevelt Institute.

Application Process

  • Open: March 9, 2026
  • Close: April 6, 2026, at 11:59 pm
  • Interviews: Mid-April for finalists
  • Final Decisions: Mid-May

Required Application Materials:

  • CV
  • Transcript confirming current enrollment in a U.S. humanities graduate program (master’s or doctoral level)
  • Writing sample
  • Short responses to 3 application questions

Eligibility Criteria:

Applicants must affirm all the following:

  1. At least 18 years old
  2. Legally authorized to work in the U.S.
  3. No visa sponsorship required for the internship duration
  4. Graduate student in good standing in the humanities at a U.S.-based institution
  5. Eligible to work part-time while enrolled in the graduate program

Apply Today

The Roosevelt Institute Graduate Humanities Internship is your chance to gain hands-on experience in public policy while leveraging your humanities training. Build professional skills, engage with meaningful projects, and prepare for a career that bridges scholarship and public service.

Apply Now

Discover more similar opportunities here. 

The post Roosevelt Institute Graduate Humanities Internship 2026–2027: Paid Remote Opportunity for Policy, Research and Public Service Careers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Green Opportunities Fund 2026: £2.2 Million Grant Supporting Youth Green Careers in the UK
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The Green Opportunities Fund is a major £2.2 million funding initiative designed to support young people across the United Kingdom in exploring and accessing career pathways within the green economy. This fund is a collaborative effort between the Co-op Foundation and Co-op, reflecting a shared commitment to building sustainable communities while ensuring equal access to […]

The post Green Opportunities Fund 2026: £2.2 Million Grant Supporting Youth Green Careers in the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Green Opportunities Fund is a major £2.2 million funding initiative designed to support young people across the United Kingdom in exploring and accessing career pathways within the green economy. This fund is a collaborative effort between the Co-op Foundation and Co-op, reflecting a shared commitment to building sustainable communities while ensuring equal access to emerging opportunities in the rapidly evolving green sector.

About the Green Opportunities Fund

The Green Opportunities Fund has been established to address a growing need: preparing the next generation for careers that contribute to environmental sustainability. As global economies shift toward greener practices, there is an increasing demand for skilled individuals who can contribute to sectors such as renewable energy, environmental conservation, sustainable agriculture, and green technology.

Recognizing this need, the Co-op Foundation partnered with Co-op to create a funding mechanism that empowers organizations working directly with young people. The fund specifically targets initiatives that inspire, educate, and equip youth with the skills and knowledge required to thrive in green careers.

This initiative is rooted in a broader mission to foster sustainable communities where opportunities are accessible to all, regardless of background. By investing in youth-focused programs, the fund aims to bridge the gap between interest in sustainability and actual career opportunities in the green economy.

Research-Driven Approach

The development of the Green Opportunities Fund is strongly informed by research. In 2023, the Co-op Foundation published its Gen Z(ero) report, which revealed that young people are highly interested in understanding the transition to a sustainable economy. The report highlighted a strong desire among youth to learn more about green jobs and how they can be part of this transformation.

At the same time, the UK faces a significant green skills gap, with industries struggling to find qualified individuals to fill roles that support environmental sustainability. This disconnect between interest and opportunity became a key driver behind the creation of the fund.

By aligning funding priorities with research insights, the Green Opportunities Fund seeks to ensure that its impact is both relevant and effective in addressing real-world challenges.

Youth-Led Input and Governance

A distinctive feature of the Green Opportunities Fund is its commitment to youth participation in decision-making. The fund has been developed in collaboration with a group of eight young individuals known as the Environmental Collective.

This group has played a critical advisory role in shaping the fund’s priorities, ensuring that it reflects the needs, aspirations, and perspectives of young people themselves. Their involvement does not end with the design phase—they will continue to contribute to key decisions throughout the lifecycle of the funding program.

This approach reinforces the fund’s credibility and relevance, as it integrates the voices of those it is designed to benefit.

Funding Focus and Objectives

The Green Opportunities Fund is specifically aimed at supporting initiatives that influence young people aged 14 to 20. The goal is to encourage this age group to:

  • Develop practical and transferable green skills
  • Gain awareness of career opportunities within the green economy
  • Pursue pathways that lead to sustainable and environmentally focused jobs

The fund prioritizes programs that not only educate but also actively engage young people, helping them transition from awareness to action.

Organizations are encouraged to propose innovative and impactful projects that can inspire long-term interest and participation in green careers.

Eligibility Criteria

The Green Opportunities Fund is open to a range of organizations operating within the UK. Eligible applicants include:

  • Charities
  • Community Interest Companies (CICs)
  • Co-operatives
  • Community Benefit Societies

By opening the fund to diverse organizational structures, the initiative aims to reach a wide spectrum of communities and deliver localized, context-specific solutions.

Application Process

Applications for the first round of the Green Opportunities Fund are currently open. Interested organizations are encouraged to review all relevant documentation before applying, including:

  • Fund guidelines
  • Application questions
  • Theory of Development
  • AI statement

These resources are designed to help applicants understand the fund’s priorities, expectations, and evaluation criteria.

To support potential applicants, a webinar was held on Wednesday, 18th March, providing detailed insights into the fund and addressing common questions. Those who were unable to attend can access the full webinar recording to gain a comprehensive understanding of the application process.

CLICK HERE TO APPLY 

Application Deadline

All applications must be submitted by midday on Friday, 17th April. Late submissions will not be considered, so organizations are advised to plan and complete their applications well in advance of the deadline.

Support and Enquiries

Applicants who have questions or require clarification during the application process can reach out directly via email at foundation@coop.co.uk. This support ensures that organizations have the guidance they need to submit strong and competitive proposals.

Conclusion

The Green Opportunities Fund represents a significant investment in the future of both young people and the environment. By addressing the green skills gap and empowering youth to pursue sustainable careers, the fund plays a crucial role in shaping a more inclusive and environmentally responsible economy.

Through strategic partnerships, research-backed insights, and youth-led input, this initiative stands out as a forward-thinking model for funding programs that aim to create lasting social and environmental impact.

Discover similar opportunities here. 

The post Green Opportunities Fund 2026: £2.2 Million Grant Supporting Youth Green Careers in the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Call for Applications: Feminist Storytelling Grant 2026 for Writers, Filmmakers, and Artists
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The Feminist Storytelling Grant 2026 is now open for applications, offering a powerful opportunity for individuals committed to using storytelling as a tool for resistance, equality, and transformative social change. This grant is specifically designed to support women and LGBTQI+ persons who are working to reclaim narratives, challenge dominant perspectives, and amplify the voices of […]

The post Call for Applications: Feminist Storytelling Grant 2026 for Writers, Filmmakers, and Artists appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Feminist Storytelling Grant 2026 is now open for applications, offering a powerful opportunity for individuals committed to using storytelling as a tool for resistance, equality, and transformative social change. This grant is specifically designed to support women and LGBTQI+ persons who are working to reclaim narratives, challenge dominant perspectives, and amplify the voices of marginalized communities through creative expression.

About the Feminist Storytelling Grant

In many societies around the world, the voices and lived experiences of women and gender-diverse individuals are often overlooked, misrepresented, or spoken for by others. The Feminist Storytelling Grant seeks to address this imbalance by creating a platform where these individuals can tell their own stories—authentically and unapologetically.

Through this initiative, selected grantees will be empowered to produce compelling, community-centered stories that reflect real experiences, challenge inequalities, and inspire meaningful dialogue and action.

The program welcomes a wide range of creatives, including filmmakers, writers, photographers, journalists, artists, and new media creators who are passionate about feminist storytelling and social justice.

Who Can Apply?

Applicants must demonstrate a strong alignment with feminist principles and a commitment to human rights. Ideal candidates should meet the following criteria:

  • Show a firm belief in feminist values and advocacy for equality.
  • Demonstrate the ability to creatively capture and present the voices and lived experiences of marginalized communities.
  • Be committed to ethical, accountable, and community-centered storytelling practices.

This opportunity is open to individuals across various creative disciplines who are dedicated to producing impactful narratives that contribute to social transformation.

Accepted Storytelling Formats

The grant supports a diverse range of storytelling formats, allowing applicants to choose the medium that best suits their creative vision. Eligible formats include:

  • Visual Storytelling: Photography, film, animation
  • Graphic & Information Design: Graphic narratives, infographics
  • Written Works: Essays, poetry, fiction, and other forms of creative writing
  • Audio Storytelling: Podcasts, radio productions
  • Performance Art: Theater and dance
  • Digital Storytelling: Interactive or digital-first narratives
  • Mixed Media: Innovative combinations of multiple formats

This flexibility ensures that storytellers can fully express their ideas using the most effective and engaging medium.

Why You Should Apply

The Feminist Storytelling Grant is more than just financial support—it is a platform for change. It provides storytellers with the resources, mentorship, and community needed to bring powerful narratives to life. If you are passionate about challenging injustice, amplifying unheard voices, and using creativity as a form of resistance, this grant offers the ideal opportunity to turn your vision into impact.

By supporting diverse storytellers and elevating authentic voices, this initiative contributes to a more inclusive and equitable global narrative landscape.

Benefits and Support for Selected Grantees

Recipients of the Feminist Storytelling Grant will gain access to a comprehensive support system designed to nurture both their creative and personal development. The program offers:

1. Mentorship

Grantees will receive professional guidance from experienced mentors who will support them throughout the storytelling process.

2. Psychological Support

Recognizing the emotional demands of storytelling—especially when dealing with sensitive or personal topics—the grant provides access to mental health and wellbeing resources.

3. Skill Development

Participants will have opportunities to enhance their technical and creative abilities through training and capacity-building sessions.

4. Community Building

Grantees will join a network of like-minded creatives, fostering collaboration, peer learning, and collective growth.

5. Exhibition Opportunities

Completed projects will be showcased through various platforms, offering visibility and recognition for the storytellers and their work.

Application Process

To apply for the Feminist Storytelling Grant, applicants must secure an endorsement from a trusted organization. Eligible endorsing bodies include:

  • Feminist network partners
  • Media collectives
  • Women Human Rights Defender (WHRD) groups
  • LGBTQI+ community organizations

This endorsement serves as a validation of the applicant’s work, credibility, and commitment to community-centered storytelling.

To Apply please click here

Deadline is 31st March 2026

Grant Timeline and Funding Details

  • Project Duration: All projects must be completed between May and October 2026, starting from the date of approval.
  • Funding Structure:
    • 50% of the grant will be disbursed upon selection.
    • The remaining 50% will be released after successful project completion.
  • Reporting Requirements:
    Grantees are required to submit:
    • Monthly progress updates
    • A final report upon project completion

All reports should be sent to the designated grant contact email provided in the application guidelines.

Discover more grant opportunities here.

The post Call for Applications: Feminist Storytelling Grant 2026 for Writers, Filmmakers, and Artists appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply for Global Engagement Lab 6 (GEL 2026–2027): Transformative Program for Grantmakers and Funders
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The Global Engagement Lab (GEL) is a unique, immersive 9-month training program designed for progressive funders and grantmakers who are ready to rethink their role in philanthropy and actively contribute to systemic change. More than a traditional capacity-building initiative, GEL is a deep learning journey that combines political education, personal transformation, and collective action. The […]

The post Apply for Global Engagement Lab 6 (GEL 2026–2027): Transformative Program for Grantmakers and Funders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Global Engagement Lab (GEL) is a unique, immersive 9-month training program designed for progressive funders and grantmakers who are ready to rethink their role in philanthropy and actively contribute to systemic change. More than a traditional capacity-building initiative, GEL is a deep learning journey that combines political education, personal transformation, and collective action.

The upcoming Global Engagement Lab 6 (GEL 6) cohort, running from September 2026 to June 2027, is centered on the theme “Organizing Funders Toward Systemic Change.” It invites participants to critically examine philanthropy as a system of power while equipping them with the tools and strategies needed to transform it from within.

Understanding the Purpose of the Global Engagement Lab

At its core, GEL is built on the recognition that today’s global challenges—climate crises, inequality, and social injustice—are deeply systemic. Addressing them requires more than traditional grantmaking approaches.

The program focuses on three foundational pillars:

  • Deepening knowledge of systemic crises and the movements responding to them
  • Fostering personal transformation to align values with action
  • Exploring the concept of the “funder organizer” as a catalyst for long-term systemic change

Through these pillars, GEL creates a space where funders can move beyond surface-level alignment and begin engaging with the deeper political and structural dimensions of their work.

Why GEL 6 Matters Now

There is growing tension within philanthropy. While many institutions speak about justice, equity, and systems change, fewer are willing to confront the realities of power, capital, and institutional risk.

GEL 6 is designed for those who recognize this gap.

Rather than offering technical solutions to improve grantmaking processes, GEL 6 challenges participants to:

  • Examine philanthropy as a site of power and influence
  • Understand the limitations of traditional funding models
  • Explore how to organize within institutions to drive meaningful change

It is a space for honest reflection, political clarity, and collective experimentation.

Key Goals of GEL 6

The program aims to equip participants with the mindset and tools needed to transform philanthropy from within. Specifically, GEL 6 seeks to:

  • Support funders in becoming active agents of change, capable of influencing both their organizations and the broader sector
  • Build knowledge, skills, and political awareness around power, capital, and narrative
  • Strengthen the ability to apply organizing principles across personal, organizational, and systemic levels
  • Move beyond values alignment toward sustained, strategic, and movement-driven action

Who Should Apply?

GEL 6 is designed for:

  • Mid-career and senior funders or grantmakers
  • Professionals who already recognize the limitations of traditional philanthropy
  • Individuals with the capacity to influence decision-making within their institutions
  • Those willing to engage deeply with complex issues of power, privilege, and systemic change

This is not an introductory course. It is intended for practitioners ready to treat philanthropy as a political actor capable of transformation.

What Participants Will Explore

The GEL 6 curriculum is structured around four interconnected areas of inquiry:

1. Personal Thresholds and Political Clarity

Participants reflect on their positionality within systems of power and capital. This includes examining personal values, risk tolerance, and the internal barriers that influence decision-making. The goal is to build more accountable and grounded leadership.

2. Funder Organizing

GEL 6 redefines the role of funders—not just as supporters of movements, but as organizers within philanthropy. Participants learn principles such as base-building, collective strategy, and long-term power-building, and explore how to apply them within their institutions.

3. Systems Change, Capital, and Narrative

The program introduces frameworks like Just Transition, Buen Vivir, and feminist and critical race theories. Participants analyze how capital and narratives shape global systems and explore ways to organize resources for transformative impact.

4. Time, Discipline, and Resilience

Systemic change requires endurance. This component focuses on long-term strategy, leadership development, institutional memory, and navigating conflict with resilience and clarity.

Learning Approach and Experience

GEL 6 uses a cohort-based, experiential learning model that integrates:

  • Reflective practice
  • Applied systems thinking
  • Peer learning and collaboration
  • Engagement with movement practitioners

Participants are expected to actively contribute, share experiences, and learn collectively. This collaborative environment fosters solidarity, accountability, and long-term alignment.

Program Structure and Timeline

The program combines in-person retreats, virtual workshops, and ongoing cohort engagement.

Key Components:

  • In-Person Retreat I (September 2026, Mexico)
  • Virtual Workshops and Monthly Check-ins
  • In-Person Retreat II (2027, location TBC – Morocco, Senegal, or Kenya)
  • Final Reflection and Continued Engagement through EDGE

Timeline Overview:

2026

  • March–April: Applications open
  • May: Cohort selection
  • June–August: Onboarding and preparation
  • September: First in-person retreat
  • October–November: Virtual sessions

2027

  • January–March: Continued workshops
  • May: Second retreat and EDGE Conference
  • June: Program closure and next steps

All virtual sessions are scheduled at 2:00 PM UTC.

Program Fees

Fees vary depending on organizational budget and EDGE membership status.

EDGE Members:

  • $3,500 – Solidarity rate (under $1M budget)
  • $5,000 – Full cost ($1M–$2M budget)
  • $6,500 – Redistribution rate (above $2M budget)

Non-Members:

  • $4,000 – Solidarity rate
  • $5,500 – Full cost
  • $7,000 – Redistribution rate

Fees cover all program-related expenses except flights.

Facilitators and Learning Community

GEL 6 will be led by experienced facilitators Bridget Brehen and Hana ElSafoury, alongside guest speakers from global movements and philanthropic organizations. Participants will also engage with a broader ecosystem of aligned initiatives such as:

  • Justice Funders
  • Thousand Currents

These partnerships reflect GEL’s commitment to collaboration rather than competition within the field of funder organizing.

What Makes GEL 6 Unique?

GEL 6 stands out for its politically grounded and action-oriented approach. Unlike traditional philanthropy training programs, it:

  • Treats philanthropy as a space for organizing and transformation
  • Provides practical tools for institutional change
  • Encourages deep personal and political reflection
  • Connects participants to movement-led strategies and global networks

By the end of the program, participants gain:

  • Clearer understanding of their influence and limitations
  • Strategies for applying organizing principles in real-world contexts
  • A shared framework for long-term systemic impact

How to Apply

Applications for GEL 6 are open between March and April 2026, with the cohort finalized in May.

Interested candidates are encouraged to apply early and prepare for a rigorous, transformative experience that goes beyond traditional professional development.

Click Here to apply.

Deadline: May 4, 2026

The Global Engagement Lab 6 is not just a program—it is a call to action for funders who are ready to move beyond conventional philanthropy and actively engage in shaping a more just and equitable world.

For those willing to confront complexity, embrace transformation, and organize for systemic change, GEL 6 offers a rare and powerful opportunity.

Discover more similar opportunities here .

The post Apply for Global Engagement Lab 6 (GEL 2026–2027): Transformative Program for Grantmakers and Funders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: High-Level Political Forum (HLPF) 2026: Youth Applications Open to Attend UN Summit in New York (Fully Global Policy Engagement Opportunity)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Youth applications are now open for the 2026 High-Level Political Forum on Sustainable Development (HLPF), taking place from July 7–15, 2026 at United Nations Headquarters in New York City. This is a prestigious opportunity for young people in Canada to engage directly with global leaders, policymakers, and development practitioners working to advance the 2030 Agenda […]

The post High-Level Political Forum (HLPF) 2026: Youth Applications Open to Attend UN Summit in New York (Fully Global Policy Engagement Opportunity) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Youth applications are now open for the 2026 High-Level Political Forum on Sustainable Development (HLPF), taking place from July 7–15, 2026 at United Nations Headquarters in New York City.

This is a prestigious opportunity for young people in Canada to engage directly with global leaders, policymakers, and development practitioners working to advance the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development and the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

What is the High-Level Political Forum (HLPF)?

The High-Level Political Forum on Sustainable Development (HLPF) is the United Nations’ central platform for:

  • Reviewing global progress on the 2030 Agenda
  • Monitoring implementation of the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)
  • Promoting accountability and policy coordination across countries
  • Driving forward inclusive, sustainable, and evidence-based solutions

Each year, HLPF convenes:

  • Heads of State and Government
  • Ministers and UN officials
  • Civil society organizations
  • Youth leaders and innovators
  • Private sector representatives

Why You Should Apply for HLPF 2026

HLPF 2026 represents a critical moment to accelerate global action toward the SDGs as the 2030 deadline approaches.

Selected youth participants will have the opportunity to:

  • Participate in high-level policy discussions at the United Nations
  • Engage with global leaders and decision-makers
  • Contribute to international dialogue on sustainable development
  • Showcase community-driven solutions and local innovations
  • Strengthen networks with global youth advocates and organizations
  • Gain exposure to global governance and multilateral processes

Eligibility Criteria

To apply for HLPF 2026, applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Be a Canadian citizen, permanent resident, or individual with refugee status
  • Reside in Canada for the duration of the programme and event
  • Be aged 18–30 years at the time of the event
  • Demonstrate active engagement in advancing the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) through:
    • Community initiatives
    • Academic research
    • Advocacy work
    • Entrepreneurship
    • Innovative projects

Important Notes

  • Individuals temporarily in Canada (e.g., students or tourists on non-immigrant visas) are not eligible
  • Applicants living outside Canada are not eligible
  • If a U.S. visa is required, applicants must indicate this in their application
  • While support letters may be provided, applicants are responsible for meeting all U.S. entry requirements

What Makes a Strong Application?

Strong candidates typically demonstrate:

  • Clear commitment to sustainable development and SDG impact
  • Evidence of leadership and initiative
  • Experience in community or global engagement
  • A focus on equity, inclusion, and collaboration
  • Ability to translate local impact into global relevance

Application Deadline

  • Deadline: April 6, 2026 (11:45 PM)
  • Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted approximately two weeks after the deadline

How to Apply

Submit your application here:
https://forms.office.com/pages/responsepage.aspx?id=Rs4xYpvFukaie_YKxYkOFyr00ELyvRhBmoHhpGlQ_59UQjc2WFhFQkJRWkdLRUFaR0NXMVJBNTVKVi4u&origin=lprLink&route=shorturl

The High-Level Political Forum (HLPF) 2026 is one of the most influential global platforms for shaping sustainable development policy. For young leaders in Canada, this is a rare opportunity to move from local action to global influence—bringing grassroots solutions to the world stage at the United Nations.

If you are passionate about driving impact, influencing policy, and contributing to the SDGs, this is an opportunity you should not miss.

The post High-Level Political Forum (HLPF) 2026: Youth Applications Open to Attend UN Summit in New York (Fully Global Policy Engagement Opportunity) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AI Policy Lead at J-PAL (MIT): Shape the Future of AI for Social Good
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Are you passionate about using artificial intelligence to drive meaningful social impact? The Abdul Latif Jameel Poverty Action Lab (J-PAL) is hiring an AI Policy Lead to support its expanding work on AI for social good. This exciting opportunity is based at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) in Cambridge, Massachusetts, United States, and offers […]

The post AI Policy Lead at J-PAL (MIT): Shape the Future of AI for Social Good appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you passionate about using artificial intelligence to drive meaningful social impact? The Abdul Latif Jameel Poverty Action Lab (J-PAL) is hiring an AI Policy Lead to support its expanding work on AI for social good.

This exciting opportunity is based at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) in Cambridge, Massachusetts, United States, and offers a hybrid work structure.

About the Role

The AI Policy Lead will play a key role at the intersection of technology, research, and policymaking. This position focuses on ensuring that AI tools are developed, evaluated, and implemented in ways that are evidence-based and socially impactful.

Working within J-PAL’s Policy & Communications team, the successful candidate will help shape how AI is used to address global development challenges.

Key Responsibilities

The AI Policy Lead will be responsible for driving initiatives that connect cutting-edge research with real-world policy applications.

Core responsibilities include:

Leading efforts to evaluate and scale promising AI tools
Building partnerships with governments, researchers, and technology companies
Ensuring AI adoption decisions are grounded in rigorous, credible evidence
Supporting policy engagement and communication strategies
Contributing to research-driven policymaking efforts

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys working across sectors and translating research into practical solutions.

Why This Role Matters

Artificial intelligence is rapidly transforming industries and societies. Ensuring that AI is used responsibly and effectively—especially in development contexts—is critical.

This role provides a unique opportunity to influence how AI is applied to solve real-world problems, particularly in areas such as poverty reduction, public policy, and global development.

Benefits of the Role

Working at J-PAL offers a chance to collaborate with leading researchers and policymakers around the world.

Benefits include:

Exposure to cutting-edge AI and policy work
Opportunity to influence global development outcomes
Collaboration with top researchers and institutions
Hybrid work environment
Professional growth in a globally recognized research organization

Who Should Apply

This role is ideal for professionals with a strong interest in AI, public policy, and social impact.

Candidates who are passionate about bridging technology and policy, and who thrive in collaborative, research-driven environments, are encouraged to apply.

Application Details

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, and interested candidates are encouraged to apply as soon as possible.

Apply here

For more Policy Opportunities from OFY, Click here.

The post AI Policy Lead at J-PAL (MIT): Shape the Future of AI for Social Good appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: India Leadership Academy for Climate Leaders (ILACL) 2026: Transform Your Impact in Climate Leadership
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the India Leadership Academy for Climate Leaders (ILACL) 2026, a high-impact leadership programme designed to equip emerging climate leaders with the skills needed to navigate complex systems and drive meaningful change. This initiative is led by Common Purpose India in partnership with YES Foundation, with venue support from Samagata Foundation. […]

The post India Leadership Academy for Climate Leaders (ILACL) 2026: Transform Your Impact in Climate Leadership appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the India Leadership Academy for Climate Leaders (ILACL) 2026, a high-impact leadership programme designed to equip emerging climate leaders with the skills needed to navigate complex systems and drive meaningful change.

This initiative is led by Common Purpose India in partnership with YES Foundation, with venue support from Samagata Foundation.

As climate challenges become increasingly central to economic growth, public health, and social equity, this programme focuses on strengthening leadership capacity rather than technical knowledge—empowering participants to lead effectively in high-pressure, resource-constrained environments.

About the Programme

The ILACL is a 4-day, in-person leadership experience designed for individuals working across climate-related sectors.

Held in Bangalore, India, the programme brings together a diverse cohort of leaders tackling challenges in areas such as biodiversity, clean air, water, waste management, and climate innovation.

Rather than focusing on technical training, the programme emphasizes leadership development, collaboration, and systems thinking.

What You Will Gain

Participants will engage in a structured learning journey aimed at strengthening their leadership effectiveness.

Key learning outcomes include:

Developing self-awareness and clarity of purpose
Building the ability to lead across sectors and diverse stakeholder groups
Strengthening influence within complex systems
Learning from experienced social sector leaders through curated sessions
Engaging in peer learning, reflection, and real-time application

The programme is highly interactive, combining workshops, discussions, and practical exercises to ensure deep learning.

Programme Benefits

ILACL offers a unique opportunity for climate professionals to grow both personally and professionally.

Benefits include:

Immersive in-person leadership training
Access to experienced mentors and sector leaders
Peer-to-peer learning with a diverse cohort
Post-programme mentoring and alumni engagement opportunities
Enhanced leadership capacity for real-world impact

This experience is particularly valuable for those looking to step into greater leadership roles within the climate and sustainability space.

Programme Details

Dates: May 26 – May 29, 2026
Location: Bangalore, India
Format: In-person (4-day intensive programme)

The programme also includes follow-up mentoring and opportunities to reconnect with the alumni network.

Who Should Apply

This programme is ideal for high-potential individuals working in climate-related fields who are ready to take on greater leadership responsibilities.

It is especially suited for professionals working in:

Climate solutions and sustainability
Environmental policy and advocacy
Urban and rural development
Social impact and nonprofit sectors

How to Apply

Interested applicants can apply through the official link .

For more Leadership opportunities, visit OFY here.

 

The post India Leadership Academy for Climate Leaders (ILACL) 2026: Transform Your Impact in Climate Leadership appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Smithsonian National Postal Museum Paid Summer Internships 2026: Strategy, Finance & Museum Operations Experience
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for paid summer internships at the Smithsonian National Postal Museum, part of the globally recognized Smithsonian Institution. These internships offer a unique opportunity for students and recent graduates to gain hands-on experience in museum operations, strategy, finance, and administration while working in Washington, D.C., United States. About the Internship Program The […]

The post Smithsonian National Postal Museum Paid Summer Internships 2026: Strategy, Finance & Museum Operations Experience appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for paid summer internships at the Smithsonian National Postal Museum, part of the globally recognized Smithsonian Institution.

These internships offer a unique opportunity for students and recent graduates to gain hands-on experience in museum operations, strategy, finance, and administration while working in Washington, D.C., United States.

About the Internship Program

The Smithsonian National Postal Museum provides immersive internship experiences designed to give participants direct exposure to how a national museum operates behind the scenes.

Interns will work alongside professionals across departments, contributing to real projects in planning, operations, and internal systems development.

This program is ideal for individuals interested in museum studies, nonprofit management, business operations, and public sector careers.

Internship Tracks

Strategic Planning Internship

The Strategic Planning Intern will work closely with the museum’s executive team and gain insight into high-level decision-making and operational planning.

Key responsibilities include:

Supporting strategic planning and management processes
Conducting archival research and industry benchmarking
Learning about museum leadership and operations

Dates: June 22, 2026 – August 14, 2026

Eligibility:
Open to undergraduate students and recent graduates (within 12 months of graduation) with an interest in:

Museum studies
Nonprofit management
Business and fundraising
Arts management
History or government

Finance & Administration Internship

This role focuses on operational and administrative support within the museum’s Finance and Administration department.

Key responsibilities include:

Supporting internal operations and process improvements
Collaborating across departments
Assisting with the development of an internal SharePoint system for document management

Dates: June 8, 2026 – August 14, 2026

Eligibility:
Open to undergraduate students and recent graduates (within 6 months of graduation) with an interest in:

Museum studies
Business administration or management
Project management
Human resources

Internship Benefits

Participants will gain valuable professional experience in a world-renowned institution.

Benefits include:

Paid internship opportunity
Hands-on experience in museum operations and management
Exposure to executive-level planning and organizational strategy
Practical skills in research, administration, and collaboration
Networking opportunities within the Smithsonian community

Location

All internships are in-person in Washington, D.C., providing a fully immersive experience within the museum environment.

How to Apply

Learn more.

Apply via SOLAA (Program ID 2235).

For more Internship opportunities from OFY, Click here.

The post Smithsonian National Postal Museum Paid Summer Internships 2026: Strategy, Finance & Museum Operations Experience appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: British Council TESOL Scholarships 2026 (Fully Funded): Study in the UK for Teachers in Africa, Europe and Asia
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The British Council English and School Education Scholarships Scheme 2026 is a fully funded international scholarship programme designed to strengthen English language teaching (ELT) in government school systems across selected countries. Delivered in partnership with the University of Portsmouth and the University of Stirling, this programme offers 40 fully funded scholarships for experienced teachers and […]

The post British Council TESOL Scholarships 2026 (Fully Funded): Study in the UK for Teachers in Africa, Europe and Asia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The British Council English and School Education Scholarships Scheme 2026 is a fully funded international scholarship programme designed to strengthen English language teaching (ELT) in government school systems across selected countries.

Delivered in partnership with the University of Portsmouth and the University of Stirling, this programme offers 40 fully funded scholarships for experienced teachers and teacher educators to pursue a part-time, online Master’s degree in TESOL (Teaching English to Speakers of Other Languages) while continuing to work in their home countries.

Overview of the TESOL Scholarship Programme

The scholarship supports educators from:

  • Ghana
  • Kenya
  • Türkiye
  • Bangladesh
  • India
  • Sri Lanka

Participants will enroll in one of the following programmes:

  • MA TESOL (Distance Learning) – University of Portsmouth (Ghana, Kenya, Türkiye)
  • MSc TESOL (Online) – University of Stirling (Bangladesh, India, Sri Lanka)

The programme begins in September 2026 and runs part-time for up to 30 months, allowing scholars to balance work and study.

Scholarship Benefits

Each selected scholar will receive a comprehensive funding package, including:

  • Full tuition fees for the TESOL master’s programme
  • Return international flights to the UK
  • Accommodation during the UK study visit
  • Daily living stipend during the visit
  • Visa application fee reimbursement
  • Coverage of one English language test fee

Additional Programme Benefits

  • Access to world-class UK university faculty
  • Professional mentoring and academic supervision
  • International networking opportunities
  • Membership in the British Council global alumni network

UK Study Visit

All scholars will participate in a fully funded two-week academic study visit to the United Kingdom, typically scheduled for Summer 2027.

This visit includes:

  • In-person academic sessions
  • Professional collaboration with peers
  • Networking with global education experts
  • Cultural exchange experiences

Programme Structure

The TESOL master’s programmes are delivered fully online, featuring:

  • Weekly learning modules
  • Asynchronous study materials
  • Live virtual sessions (where applicable)
  • One-to-one tutor support
  • Dissertation supervision

This flexible format allows participants to remain in their home countries while advancing their qualifications.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must:

  • Be citizens and permanent residents of an eligible country
  • Work in or directly with the government education system
  • Be active English language teachers or teacher educators
  • Meet the academic requirements of the selected university
  • Demonstrate commitment to improving national ELT systems

Eligible Applicants

  • Government school teachers
  • Teacher trainers and educators
  • Contract or temporary government teachers (if within the public system)

Who Cannot Apply

  • Private-sector teachers not affiliated with government systems
  • Dual British citizens
  • British Council or UK government employees (and close relatives)
  • Individuals who studied in the UK within the past 5 years
  • Applicants currently receiving UK government funding
  • Individuals not residing in an eligible country

Application Process

Applicants must follow a two-step process:

Step 1: Apply to a TESOL Master’s Programme

  • Apply to the relevant university (Portsmouth or Stirling)
  • Receive a conditional or unconditional offer

Step 2: Apply for the Scholarship

  • Submit the scholarship application after receiving the university offer
  • Applications without a university offer will not be considered

Applicants from Ghana, Kenya, and Türkiye: University of Portsmouth MA TESOL (Distance Learning) programme. Watch the information webinar. 
And apply or enquire.

Applicants from Bangladesh, India, and Sri Lanka: University of Stirling MSc TESOL (Online) programme. Watch the information webinar. 
And apply or enquire.

Required Documents

For university admission, applicants typically need:

  • Degree certificates and academic transcripts
  • Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • Personal statement
  • Academic or professional reference
  • English language test score (if already available)

English Language Requirements

  • Not required at the initial application stage
  • Required only after shortlisting

Accepted tests include:

  • IELTS
  • TOEFL
  • Pearson
  • Cambridge English

The scholarship covers one test attempt.

Key Dates

  • Application deadline: 30 April 2026
  • Programme start: September 2026
  • Scholarship decisions: End of June 2026
  • UK study visit: Summer 2027

Duration of Study

  • University of Stirling: Up to 30 months
  • University of Portsmouth: Up to 26 months

Scholar Expectations

After completing the programme, scholars are expected to:

  • Continue working within the government education system
  • Contribute to teacher development and training
  • Strengthen English language teaching practices nationally
  • Participate in British Council alumni activities

Why Apply for the British Council TESOL Scholarships?

This fully funded scholarship provides a unique opportunity to:

  • Gain an internationally recognized TESOL master’s degree
  • Enhance teaching practice and leadership skills
  • Access global academic and professional networks
  • Contribute to national education system improvement

Learn more and apply on the official website here.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Can I apply if I already have a Master’s degree?

Yes, but priority is given to applicants without a TESOL or ELT master’s degree.

Do I need to be in my home country?

Yes. Applicants must live and work in their eligible country.

Can I transfer previous ELT qualifications?

No. Scholarship recipients must complete the full programme.

Is the programme fully online?

Yes, with one mandatory two-week UK study visit.

Final Thoughts

The British Council English and School Education Scholarships Scheme 2026 is a highly competitive, fully funded opportunity for educators committed to transforming English language teaching systems.

By combining flexible online learning, international exposure, and leadership development, this programme equips teachers with the skills and networks needed to drive meaningful educational change.

If you want, I can also create:

Learn more and apply on the official website here.

Looking for more Scholarship opportunities? Check out the Opportunities for Youth website!

The post British Council TESOL Scholarships 2026 (Fully Funded): Study in the UK for Teachers in Africa, Europe and Asia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Heidelberg Laureate Forum 2026 Journalist Travel Grant (Fully Funded to Germany)
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The Heidelberg Laureate Forum Foundation is offering an exceptional opportunity for science and technology journalists worldwide to attend the 13th Heidelberg Laureate Forum (HLF) in Germany from September 13–18, 2026. This prestigious event gathers winners of the Fields Medal, Turing Award, and Abel Prize, along with 200 of the world’s most outstanding young researchers in […]

The post Heidelberg Laureate Forum 2026 Journalist Travel Grant (Fully Funded to Germany) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Heidelberg Laureate Forum Foundation is offering an exceptional opportunity for science and technology journalists worldwide to attend the 13th Heidelberg Laureate Forum (HLF) in Germany from September 13–18, 2026.

This prestigious event gathers winners of the Fields Medal, Turing Award, and Abel Prize, along with 200 of the world’s most outstanding young researchers in mathematics and computer science. It provides a unique platform for journalists to engage directly with leading experts shaping the future of science.

🌟 About the Heidelberg Laureate Forum

The Heidelberg Laureate Forum is a globally recognized networking conference that fosters dialogue between top-tier researchers and laureates in mathematics and computer science.

Journalists selected for the program gain exclusive access to:

  • In-depth discussions on cutting-edge research

  • Direct interactions with globally recognized laureates

  • Insights into emerging scientific trends

  • A vibrant international network of experts and communicators

🎤 Special Highlight

A key highlight of the 2026 forum is the Lindau Lecture, which will be delivered by Nobel Prize-winning physicist Brian Schmidt, renowned for discovering the accelerating expansion of the universe.

💰 Travel Grant Benefits

Successful applicants will receive full financial support, including:

  • Round-trip travel to Germany

  • Accommodation throughout the forum

  • Visa costs fully covered

  • Full access to all sessions and networking opportunities

This ensures participants can focus entirely on storytelling, learning, and professional growth.

📝 Application Process

To apply for the travel grant:

  1. Create an account on the official application portal

  2. Enter your personal details (name, email, login credentials)

  3. Activate your account via the confirmation email

  4. Log in and complete your application form

  5. Submit before the deadline

Ensure your application clearly demonstrates your experience in science or technology journalism and your motivation for attending.

⏰ Application Deadline

The application period runs from March 2 to April 8, 2026.

Final Deadline: April 8, 2026

Applications submitted after this date will not be considered.

🚀 Why You Should Apply

This opportunity offers immense value for journalists looking to elevate their careers:

  • Access to exclusive, high-level scientific discussions

  • Opportunities to produce impactful global stories

  • Networking with top scientists and fellow journalists

  • Exposure to groundbreaking developments in math and computer science

🔗 How to Apply

Applicants should visit the official Heidelberg Laureate Forum website, create an account, and complete the application before the deadline.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more travel grant opportunities, click here. 

The post Heidelberg Laureate Forum 2026 Journalist Travel Grant (Fully Funded to Germany) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
